channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Building an indexable verification page for a freshly-launched small business
item.description: Building an indexable verification page for a freshly-launched small business site When a small business launches a new website, Google treats it as untrusted for 30 to 90 days. New domain. No...

item.title: FinancialService schema for a real merchant services brokerage: a case study
item.description: FinancialService schema for a real merchant services brokerage: a case study Schema.org gives you FinancialService as a type. Most sites that use it stop there. Combined with LocalBusiness,...

item.title: How Free Online Tools Survive Without Collecting Your Email
item.description: The question comes up every time someone recommends a free, no-account tool: "How does this even stay online? How are they paying for servers?" It's a fair question. Free tools have to exist...

item.title: The Day the Treasure Hunt Engine Buried Itself Alive
item.description: The Problem We Were Actually Solving Our first production spike came during a Black Friday weekend when the hunt feed exceeded 180k concurrent sessions. The Rails process memory ballooned to 4.2...

item.description: Zero-Day Exploits, GitHub Actions Supply Chain Attacks, and OTP Auth Flaws Today's Highlights This week's top security news features a critical zero-day actively exploited in KnowledgeDeliver LMS...

item.title: Only 14.6% of 'AI-native' job postings actually name an AI tool. I checked 37,920.
item.description: Canonical: this is a cross-post. The original lives at https://four-leaf.ai/blog/what-ai-native-means-in-job-postings Every company in tech calls itself AI-native, and as a label it's useless...

item.title: From "Vibe Coding" to Precision: Why GitHub Spec Kit Changes Everything
item.description: If you've ever felt like your AI coding assistant is just guessing what you want to build, it's time to flip the script. Code has been king for decades, with specifications often discarded once...

item.title: Why the Vatican Invited Anthropic to the Pope’s AI Encyclical Presentation
item.description: Pope Leo’s first encyclical marks an unprecedented alliance between the Church and Silicon Valley.

item.title: What Pope Leo XIV’s First Encyclical Says About the Power of AI
item.description: In Magnifica Humanitas, the Pope decries the concentration of technological power in a few global players.

item.title: Google Health is here, but a lot of people want their Fitbit app back instead
item.description: The Fitbit app is no more. Along with the launch of the new Fitbit Air (which you can expect a full review of once we've spent more time with it), Google has officially replaced it with Google...

item.title: GE’s nugget ice maker is nearly half off if you buy it refurbished
item.description: If you’re looking for ways to make staying cool and hydrated easier this summer, Woot is currently selling a refurbished GE Profile Opal 2.0 Ultra Nugget Ice Maker for $264.99 through June 3rd....

item.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
item.description: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

item.title: Sony’s sloppy Spider-Man universe gets even messier with Spider-Noir
item.description: After years of it seeming like the Spider-Man film rights might be better off in Marvel's hands alone, Into the Spider-Verse came along and proved that Sony was still capable of telling phenomenal...

item.title: The best Memorial Day sales you can still shop
item.description: The long weekend is over, but many of the best Memorial Day deals are still available. Many of our favorite portable speakers are discounted, as are other outdoorsy products we like, including a...

item.title: American Airlines is getting Starlink Wi-Fi
item.description: American Airlines is planning to install SpaceX's Starlink Wi-Fi in hundreds of its airplanes, the airline announced today. American says the deployment will start in the first quarter of 2027,...

item.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
item.description: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training...

item.title: Memory V re-creates the Memorymoog without the massive headaches or price tag
item.description: The Memorymoog is legendary for being an absolutely massive-sounding synth and being incredibly unreliable. But now you can enjoy its classic Moog sound without the headaches or the sky-high...

item.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
item.description: The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026. The...

item.title: Saving for a Switch 2 is easier with Newegg’s gift card deal
item.description: If you foresee a bunch of Nintendo purchases in your future, Newegg’s deal on Nintendo gift cards might just help save your wallet. The retailer is offering a deal that lets gamers get $200 worth...

item.title: Oppo’s Bubble is a thin round screen for taking rear camera selfies
item.description: Oppo launched a new smartphone accessory that makes it easier to snap selfies using your smartphone's rear cameras that typically feature better sensors than front-facing cameras. The Bubble...

item.title: Govee included a book on ‘white supremacy’ in its website imagery
item.description: Companies often include background props in their product imagery that serve as set dressing, but one of Govee's decorative choices is raising some eyebrows. An eagle-eyed Verge reader spotted...

item.title: Identifying People Using Wi-Fi Routers
item.description: Not identifying people based on their use of Wi-Fi routers, but identifying people using Wi-Fi signals. This is accomplished through what is known as WiFi sensing, or the use of WiFi signals to...

item.title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
item.description: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies.

item.title: Rethinking organizational design in the age of agentic AI
item.description: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution. Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next...

item.title: Jony Ive’s Ferrari looks nothing like a Ferrari
item.description: Ferrari makes some of the fastest cars on the planet, so it's anyone's guess how the Italian automaker ended up being so incredibly late to the EV party. Long after most automakers have...

item.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
item.description: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.

item.title: The Download: puncturing the AI jobs panic
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. A reality check on the AI jobs hysteria Despite the...
